quote: Design for failure.
author: Werner Vogels

quote: Simplicity scales better than complexity.
author: Unknown

quote: Everything fails, all the time.
author: Werner Vogels

quote: A distributed system is one where the failure of a computer you didn't even know existed can render your own computer unusable.
author: Leslie Lamport

quote: There are only two hard things in Computer Science: cache invalidation and naming things.
author: Phil Karlton

quote: Premature optimization is the root of all evil.
author: Donald Knuth

quote: Make it work, make it right, make it fast.
author: Kent Beck

quote: The best architectures emerge from self-organizing teams.
author: Agile Manifesto

quote: You can't sacrifice partition tolerance, so you're always choosing between consistency and availability.
author: Eric Brewer

quote: Microservices are not a silver bullet.
author: Martin Fowler

quote: The key to performance is elegance, not battalions of special cases.
author: Jon Bentley

quote: Scalability is not about speed. It's about handling growth.
author: Unknown

quote: Design systems that are easy to delete, not easy to extend.
author: Tef

quote: Every system eventually becomes a distributed system.
author: Unknown

quote: Redundancy is the key to reliability.
author: Unknown

quote: If you can't measure it, you can't improve it.
author: Peter Drucker

quote: Good architecture makes the system easy to change.
author: Robert C. Martin

quote: Latency is the silent killer of user experience.
author: Unknown

quote: The network is unreliable — design accordingly.
author: Fallacies of Distributed Computing

quote: Consistency is a spectrum, not a binary.
author: Unknown

quote: An idempotent operation can be applied multiple times without changing the result beyond the initial application.
author: Unknown

quote: Horizontal scaling beats vertical scaling for long-term growth.
author: Unknown

quote: Event-driven architectures decouple producers from consumers.
author: Unknown

quote: Load balancers are the traffic cops of distributed systems.
author: Unknown

quote: Rate limiting protects your system from abuse and cascading failures.
author: Unknown
